D7 Print L2 Understand Atom

Paper Stock Tour

coated stock holds ink and snaps the colours; uncoated drinks a little; newsprint drinks a lot and lends its own warm tint to everything

The paper is half of the print. Coated stocks (gloss, matt) have a clay-filled surface that holds the ink on top, giving deep blacks and saturated colours. Uncoated stocks absorb a little ink — colours sit softer. Newsprint absorbs a lot, gains dots, and its own yellowish tint shows through every highlight.

Each column prints the same swatches on a different stock. Raise the exaggeration to make the small real differences obvious: cooler/warmer paper white, deeper or softer shadows, more or less ink gain across the midtones.
